description_long: This is a vintage-style megaphone with a large, flared horn made of matte black material, reminiscent of early 20th-century audio devices. The horn curves outward from a circular base, which is supported by a sturdy ring that likely provides stability when placed on a surface. A visible cable extends from the base, suggesting it is electrically powered rather than battery-operated. The mouthpiece is rounded and smoothly transitions into the main body, with no visible grill or mesh at the horn's opening. The surface appears hard and smooth, with a subtle sheen but not highly reflective. There is a small emblem or logo at the center of the horn's interior, but no other visible markings or controls.
description: A matte black, vintage-style megaphone with a large flared horn, circular base, and a visible power cable. The surface is smooth and hard, with minimal visible controls or branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full curved horn, circular base, and the cable extending from the bottom.
description_view_1: Front view directly into the horn, revealing the flared opening and a small emblem or logo at the center.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, highlighting the smooth curve of the horn and the base ring, with the cable partially visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the horn and the circular base, with the cable extending outward.
attribute_mouthpiece_design: Rounded, smooth, and integrated into the main body with no distinct separation or covering.
attribute_branding_or_logo: Small emblem or logo visible at the center of the horn's interior.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
